title: Trichotosia brevirachis
taxonomy: Trichotosia brevirachis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Trichotosia, which is a group of orchids that are mainly found in Southeast Asia. Within the Orchidaceae family, Trichotosia brevirachis is further classified as a member of the subfamily Epidendroideae. This subfamily is a large and diverse group of orchids that includes many species of epiphytic and terrestrial orchids. The classification of Trichotosia brevirachis reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other orchids.
uses: Trichotosia brevirachis is a plant species found in Southeast Asia, and it has been traditionally used in folk medicine. Its roots and stems are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
